result:
Confirmed via `checkType` → `addReplyErrorObject(c, shared.wrongtypeerr)` (the actual reply-object construction, shared across all type-checked commands). Cross-checked with `explore`/`callers` and direct file reads at `src/object.c:884-891`.

## Answer

The WRONGTYPE error *object* itself is built once, generically, in `checkType()`:

- **`src/object.c:884-891`** — `checkType(client *c, robj *o, int type)`
  ```c
  if (o && o->type != type) {
      addReplyErrorObject(c, shared.wrongtypeerr);   // line 887 — the actual reply write
      return 1;
  }
  ```
  This is the one and only place `shared.wrongtypeerr` is sent to the client. It's a shared helper called by ~117 commands (list/hash/set/zset/stream/etc.), so it is *not* SET-specific by itself — what makes it "the SET path" is which call site invokes it.

For the **SET command path specifically**, there are two distinct call sites that can trigger this reply, both reached only from `setCommand`/`setGenericCommand` in `src/t_string.c`:

1. **`src/t_string.c:119`** — inside `setGenericCommand()` itself (defined at `t_string.c:87`), guarding the `IFEQ`/`IFNE`/`IFDEQ`/`IFDNE` conditional-SET flags:
   ```c
   kvobj *current = lookupKeyRead(c->db, key);
   if (checkType(c, current, OBJ_STRING)) {   // line 119
       return;
   }
   ```
   This fires when `SET key value IFEQ ...` (etc.) targets an existing key that isn't a string.

2. **`src/t_string.c:467`** — inside `getGenericCommand()` (defined at `t_string.c:461`), which `setGenericCommand` calls at **`t_string.c:100`** only when the `GET` flag is set (`SET key value GET`):
   ```c
   if (checkType(c, o, OBJ_STRING)) {   // line 467
       return C_ERR;
   }
   ```
   `getGenericCommand` is shared with `GET`/`GETDEL`/`GETSET`, but reaching it via `setGenericCommand` is the SET-specific route.

Call chain summary:
```
setCommand (t_string.c:443)
 └─ setGenericCommand (t_string.c:87)
     ├─ line 100: getGenericCommand (t_string.c:461)   [only if SET ... GET]
     │              └─ line 467: checkType → addReplyErrorObject(shared.wrongtypeerr)  [object.c:887]
     └─ line 119: checkType (direct)                   [only if SET ... IFEQ/IFNE/IFDEQ/IFDNE]
                    └─ addReplyErrorObject(shared.wrongtypeerr)  [object.c:887]
```

Note: a plain `SET key value` with no `GET`/`IF*` flags never calls `checkType` — SET unconditionally overwrites any existing type, so WRONGTYPE from SET only occurs via one of these two guarded flag paths.
